title: Finite time blowup for an averaged three-dimensional Navier-Stokes equation

conjecture:1.1.summary: Classical formulation: smooth solutions with finite energy exist globally for Schwartz initial data.

conjecture:1.2.summary: Mild solution formulation: global mild solution in $H^10_df$ exists for Schwartz initial data.

thm:1.5.summary: There exists a symmetric averaged Euler bilinear operator and Schwartz initial data such that no global mild solution exists. THE MAIN THEOREM.

def:3.1.summary: Bilinear operators built from Littlewood-Paley-type projections at dyadic scales, acting on divergence-free fields.

thm:3.2.summary: For small enough dyadic parameter, every local cascade operator is an averaged Euler bilinear operator. Proved via 9 steps of harmonic analysis (Sections 3.1-3.9).

thm:3.3.summary: There exists a symmetric local cascade operator and initial data with no global mild solution. Combined with Thm 3.2, this gives the main theorem.

def:3.4.summary: Formalization of what it means for one bilinear operator to be a complex average of another, via rotations, dilations, and Fourier multipliers.

lem:4.1.summary: Reduces the PDE (cascade equation) to an infinite-dimensional ODE system in coefficients $X_{i,n}$ and energies $E_{i,n}$, with a priori regularity, energy defect control, and frequency localization.

thm:4.2.summary: There exist structure constants such that for large enough $n_0$, no continuously differentiable functions can satisfy the ODE system from Lemma 4.1. The ODE-level blowup result.

prop:5.1.summary: Finite time blowup for a simplified dyadic model with exogenous truncation. Warmup for the full argument — shows energy cascading to finer scales via pump gates.

thm:5.3.summary: The 5-mode circuit (a,b,c,d,ã) with pump + amplifier + rotor gates produces a delayed but abrupt transfer of energy from a to ã at a critical time $t_c$. THIS IS THE CORE MECHANISM that Section 6 iterates at every scale.

thm:6.2.summary: For appropriate parameters (K large, $\varepsilon$ small, $n_0$ large), the ODE system has no global solution. Direct implication of Thm 4.2 with explicit structure constants.

prop:6.3.summary: The main inductive proposition. Energy concentrates at scale n at time $t_n$, then transfers to scale n+1 by time $t_{n+1}$. Transition states and energy bounds are maintained inductively.

prop:6.4.summary: Given Prop 6.3 at level N, produces the N+1 case. The inductive step. Reduces to the rescaled Prop 6.5.

prop:6.5.summary: The rescaled version of the inductive step. Works with normalized variables $(a_k,b_k,c_k,d_k)$ at scale 0. THIS IS THE TECHNICAL HEART — everything in Sections 6.4-6.7 proves this.

lem:6.7.summary: Integral of energy at any fixed scale over the history is bounded. Uses energy estimates from Prop 6.5 and geometric series.

lem:6.8.summary: Establishes improved initial bounds at time 0 for the rescaled system, using transition state from Prop 6.5 and cumulative energy from Lem 6.7.

lem:6.9.summary: Energy at each scale is essentially captured by the four primary modes (a,b,c,d), up to small viscosity error. Uses energy defect (eq:6.51) and Lem 6.7.

lem:6.10.summary: Rules out energy escaping to distant scales. Uses initial bounds (Lem 6.8), energy inequality (eq:6.49), and energy estimates.

cor:6.11.summary: At the exit time $T_1$, exactly one of three things happens: energy flows backward, energy flows forward (via $d_1)$, or we run out of time. Follows from Lem 6.10.

prop:6.12.summary: First reduction of what needs to be proved: find $\tau_1$ and $\mu_1$ satisfying the transition state bounds. Assuming the 'running out the clock' exit.

prop:6.13.summary: If the integral of $a_1^2$ is small, then $b_1, c_1, d_1$ remain small. Controls subsidiary modes when the primary mode hasn't activated yet. Uses Lem 6.7, Lem 6.8, Gronwall.

cor:6.14.summary: Refined exit trichotomy after eliminating the $b_1,c_1,d_1$ contributions. Uses Prop 6.13 to simplify Cor 6.11.

prop:6.15.summary: Second reduction: strengthened transition state bounds. Assuming the 'forwards flow' or 'running out the clock' exit from Cor 6.14.

prop:6.16.summary: Reduces the trichotomy to a dichotomy: either energy flows backward or we run out the clock. The forwards flow case has been handled.

prop:6.17.summary: Rules out backward energy flow using modified energy / equipartition argument (parallel to Thm 5.3's proof). Forces 'running out the clock', which completes the induction.

Base case is direct; inductive step is Prop 6.4

Lemma 6.8 is proved within Prop 6.5's hypothetical framework, sharing its transition-state and energy estimates; not a dependency on Prop 6.5's conclusion.

layer_1_main_reduction.description: Main theorem splits into two independent parts

layer_2_averaging.description: 9-step harmonic analysis argument showing cascade operators are averaged Euler operators

layer_3_pde_to_ode.description: Reduction from cascade PDE to infinite-dimensional ODE system

layer_4_circuit_design.description: Quadratic circuits: pump, amplifier, rotor gates produce delayed abrupt energy transition

layer_5_induction_framework.description: Multi-scale induction: energy cascades through scales, each step is a rescaled copy of the circuit

layer_6_technical_core.description: The 7-step proof of the rescaled inductive step — the technical heart of the paper
